items.title: Big Picture: Getting to Know Rivers
items.description: Start your journey by understanding what rivers are, why they matter to our lives and culture, and how pollution enters these living systems.

topics.label: What is a river as a living ecosystem?
Why clean rivers matter to daily life and spirituality
The journey of pollution—how a river turns dirty
Different types of pollution: visible and invisible

items.title: Main Sources of Pollution: Everyday Language
items.description: Explore the biggest pollution sources—sewage, plastic, industrial waste, farm chemicals, and construction—explained in simple, everyday terms.

topics.label: Where sewage goes after you flush
Farm fertilizers and pesticides washing into rivers

items.title: Science Made Simple: How We Measure River Health
items.description: Learn key water quality indicators like dissolved oxygen (DO), BOD, pH, and more using everyday comparisons—no chemistry degree required.

topics.label: Key parameters: DO and BOD explained simply

items.title: People, Culture, and Power Structures
items.description: Understand the cultural significance of holy rivers, who is responsible for protection, and India's environmental laws in clear language.

items.title: Case Studies: Real Rivers, Real Stories
items.description: Real examples from Ganga and Yamuna, peninsular rivers, urban nallas, plus inspiring global success stories of polluted rivers revived.

items.title: Technology, Data, and Citizen Action
items.description: Modern tools like satellites, apps, and sensors plus practical steps you can take to monitor, protect, and restore rivers.

topics.label: Remote sensing and GIS for rivers
Adopting and documenting a river stretch
Vision of a restored river future
